items.title: Should You Rethink Travelers Stock After Recent 4.7% Pullback?
items.description: If you’re thinking about what to do next with Travelers Companies stock, you’re not alone. Despite a recent pullback, with shares down 4.7% in the past week and off 2.5% over the past month, Travelers has shown its staying power. The stock is up 11.9% year-to-date and has gained an impressive 138.9% over five years. It’s the kind of steady story that makes investors wonder if the latest price drop is just noise or a new opportunity. Even with shifting trends in the insurance sector and...

items.title: TSMC earnings, Fed comments, homebuilder data: What to Watch
items.description: Market Domination Overtime host Josh Lipton previews several of the biggest stories to come tomorrow, Thursday, October 16, including corporate earnings from companies like Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM) and Charles Schwab (SCHW), commentary from Federal Reserve officials, and October's homebuilder confidence reading. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination Overtime.

items.title: Progressive Stock’s Slide May Have Gone Too Far. It’s a Chance to Buy.
items.description: Wall Street may have overreacted to bad news at Progressive, slamming the stock and sending shares lower across the auto insurance industry.  The problem was that Progressive, tops among U.S. property and casualty insurers with a market value of $130 billion, reported a profit of 52 cents a share for September, down 48% from the year-earlier period.  Its combined ratio—the sum of expenses and claims as a percentage of premiums—came in 86.5% for September, excluding a $950 million so-called policyholder-credit expense for Florida customers, according to the company.

items.title: Markets Mostly Up, Russell 2000 Sets New Closing High
items.description: The biggest movers on the day were the Nasdaq, +148 points or +0.66%, and the small-cap Russell 2000 +24 points, +0.97%.

items.title: This Insurer Is the One of the Worst Stocks in the S&P 500 Today. Here’s Why.
items.description: Progressive posts weaker-than-expected earnings for the third quarter due in part to a policy change in Florida.
